transportOccuptionTargDrawHeading: Category-Based Express Entry Selection Draws: Transport Occupations
transportOccuptionTargDrawPara: The Transport Occupations category encompasses a range of vital roles essential for the smooth functioning of the transport sector. These roles include aircraft assemblers, transport truck drivers, railway traffic controllers, marine traffic regulators, air traffic controllers, pilots, aircraft mechanics, railway carmen/women, and transportation managers.
EligibilityFirstHeading: First You Must Be Eligible with Express Entry
EligFirstSubHead: Before you can participate in a STEM-specific draw through Canada's Express Entry system, you need to be eligible. Here's how you can get started:
EligPara1: Join the Express Entry Pool: The first step towards becoming a candidate for a STEM-specific draw is to get into the Express Entry pool.
EligPara2: Choose Your Program: There are three programs you can be eligible for to join the Express Entry pool:
EligPara2Li1: This program is ideal for tech workers who have the necessary work experience, education, and language ability.
EligPara2Li2: Like the FSW program, the CEC is also suitable for tech workers who have gained Canadian work experience.
EligPara2Li3: If you're a trades worker, this program is designed for you. It requires proof of relevant work experience, education, and language proficiency.
EligPara3: Remember, each program has its own set of eligibility requirements. So, make sure to review these carefully and ensure you meet all the necessary criteria before applying.
EligCritHeading: Eligibility Criteria for Transport Occupations Category
EligCritSubHead: Once you have met eligibility for one of the Express Entry programs, now you need to meet eligibility for Transport specific criteria:
ec1: Within the last 3 years, you have at least 6 months of full-time, continuous work experience (or an equivalent amount of part-time work experience) in a single eligible transport occupation. This experience can be gained either in Canada or abroad.
etroT1Li1: Aircraft assemblers and aircraft assembly inspectors
etroT1Li3: Railway traffic controllers and marine traffic regulators
etroT1Li6: Air traffic controllers and related occupations
etroT1Li7: Air pilots, flight engineers and flying instructors
ExpressEntryPara: The first-ever Express Entry draw for Transport occupation workers was held in September 2023. Express Entry draw #264 saw a CRS score of 435 and invited 1000 candidates eligible for category-based selection in Transport occupations.

q1: If I have a targeted Transport Occupation, does my CRS score still matter?
qa1: Your CRS score remains crucial even with a targeted Transport occupation. HAt Brightlight Immigration, we have a dedicated team of visa application specialists who can assist you from the start of the application process all the way to obtaining your PR. Start your process now. However, in Transport - specific draws, the CRS score needed to secure an invitation to apply (ITA) tends to be lower compared to comprehensive program draws. For example, in the inaugural Transport -specific draw, the CRS cutoff stood at 435, notably lower than the 524 required in concurrent all-program draws.
q2: If I have a targeted Trade Occupation, does my CRS score still matter?
qa2: There is no set schedule of Transport-specific draws by IRCC. However, IRCC has indicated a commitment to prioritize Transport-specific draws in the future, although no specific timeline has been announced.
q3: Do I need to provide extra documents if I am selected in a Transport- Specific draw?
qa3: Additional documents are not required; however, it's essential to ensure that the provided work experience documents distinctly display a minimum of 6 months of Transport experience within the past 3 years. This experience can be gained either in Canada or abroad.
q4: My spouse qualifies for a Transport-specific draw, can my profile receive the invitation to apply (ITA)?
qa4: In a Transport-specific draw, the principal applicant must possess the required experience to receive an invitation to apply (ITA). If your spouse meets the criteria, they should create their own Express Entry profile where they are listed as the principal applicant.
